methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $63M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $100M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring

private.name: Zeta Surgical

data.narrative:
Medra is a Series A biotech & health company founded by Michelle Lee that has raised $63M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 11 peers: 3 public companies (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private firms including Eikon Therapeutics, Neko Health, New Limit, Asimov, and Profluent, predominantly at Series B stage.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis uses the $63M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $126M. Peer-median analysis across 6 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a median valuation of $805M, with a range spanning $100M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (7.4x median across the three public tickers) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $107M (low) to $926M (high), with a median of $466M. The substantial spread reflects inherent uncertainty in early-stage biotech valuations and the stage mismatch between Medra (Series A) and most peers (Series B). Stage-progression yields more conservative guidance; peer medians reflect market pricing but include companies with potentially superior clinical or commercial traction. Key uncertainties include Medra's competitive position, pipeline maturity, and burn trajectory relative to the peer set.
